name: TihńĀmi QaŠł•tńĀni Arabic

ancestors.notes: Peninsular Arabic base ‚ÄĒ most conservative; TihńĀmi QaŠł•tńĀni is a lowland/TihńĀmah southwestern variety of the same conservative highland-TihńĀmah type as its neighbour ar-SA-x-rijal-alma, but with a TihńĀmi-lowland consonant system (voiced qńĀf, «ßńęm‚Üí[ í]) and a ŠłćńĀd/ŠļďńĀ ĺ merger in progress.

notes: TihńĀmi QaŠł•tńĀni Arabic (ŔĄŔáō¨ō© ō™ŔáōßŔÖō© Ŕāō≠ō∑ōßŔÜ), the sedentary Arabic of the QaŠł•ŠĻ≠ńĀn tribe in TihńĀmat QaŠł•ŠĻ≠ńĀn, the coastal-plain/escarpment zone of  ŅAsńęr in southwestern Saudi Arabia (villages al-Jawwa in the highlands, al-FarŇ°a in the lowlands). It is a NEIGHBOUR of ‚ÄĒ and NOT identical to ‚ÄĒ RijńĀl Alma Ņ (ar-SA-x-rijal-alma): both belong to the conservative  ŅAsńęr/TihńĀmah type and both preserve a reflex of the ancient lateral ŠłćńĀd, but they differ systematically. MODELED HERE, following K. Alqahtani (2015, Essex PhD, the primary monograph): (1) qńĀf Ŕā ‚Üí VOICED velar […°] (TihńĀmi lowland reflex; gamar 'moon', gaňźm 'he stood', galam 'pen'), NOT the qńĀf-retaining [q] of highland-sedentary RijńĀl Alma Ņ ‚ÄĒ this is the single sharpest split between the two lects. (2) «ßńęm ō¨ ‚Üí voiced post-alveolar fricative [ í] ( íabal 'mountain',  íamal 'camel',  íim ēa 'Friday'), NOT the affricate [d í] of the peninsular parent / RijńĀl Alma Ņ. (3) ŠłćńĀd ō∂ ‚Üí the archaic voiced emphatic lateral fricative […ģň§] as the conservative reflex, DRIFTING to the emphatic interdental [√įň§]: Alqahtani (2015) documents a MERGER IN PROGRESS in which […ģň§] and [√įň§] alternate on both etymological *Šłć and *Šļď words, led by younger women toward [√įň§] (merger formula /…ģň§/+/√įň§/‚Üí/√įň§/, p.170). This is the crucial contrast with RijńĀl Alma Ņ, where Watson & Al-Azraqi (2011) report *Šłć (voiced lateral […ģň§]) and *Šļď (VOICELESS lateral […¨ň§]) kept DISTINCT; TihńĀmi QaŠł•tńĀni has NO voiceless lateral […¨ň§] ‚ÄĒ its ŠļďńĀ ĺ reflex is the voiced emphatic interdental [√įň§] ( ēa√įň§m 'bone', wa√įň§iňźfa 'job'). (4) ŠļďńĀ ĺ ōł ‚Üí [√įň§] (emphatic interdental), no lateral. INHERITED and consistent with the southwestern conservative type: interdentals ōę /őł/ ōį /√į/ retained; emphatic pharyngealization backing low vowels (AR_PEN_EMPH_*); long [uňź] preserved. Additional TihńĀmi features NOT modelled at grapheme level (they are lexical/morphological and belong downstream): the m-definite article (m-article ~ l-article variation, a salient ancient TihńĀmi feature), imńĀla (aňź‚Üíeňź near /i/, aňź‚Üíoňź in a small lexical set), and pausal apocope. NO ar-SA umbrella node (Saudi dialects do not form a pan-Saudi phonological group); this leaf parents directly to ar-x-peninsular, as its sibling ar-SA-x-rijal-alma does. INPUT CONTRACT: transcribes orthography+harakat; short vowels surface only when the input is vocalised. STUB/RESEARCH scope: grapheme-level consonant reflexes are modelled and cited; the in-progress ŠłćńĀd/ŠļďńĀ ĺ merger is represented as the […ģň§]~[√įň§] candidate pair (conservative lateral first) rather than as a stable phonemic split, and the sociolinguistic variation is left to a downstream lexicon/rescorer.

sources.author: Alqahtani, Khairiah
sources.title: A sociolinguistic study of the Tihami Qahtani dialect in Asir, Southern Arabia (PhD thesis)
sources.notes: The primary monograph on TihńĀmi QaŠł•tńĀni. Consonant inventory (Table 2.1, p.45): qńĀf ‚Üí […°] (p.46: gamar, gaňźm, galam, galiňźl); «ßńęm ‚Üí [ í] (p.46:  íabal,  íamal,  íim ēa); interdentals őł/√į/√įň§ retained; plain /k/ (no affrication). ŠłćńĀd: conservative voiced emphatic lateral fricative […ģň§] (p.45: …ģň§iyuňźf 'guests', …ģň§aňźn 'lamb', ha…ģň§ ēart 'I attended') MERGING with emphatic interdental [√įň§] on both etymological sets (pp.46, 149-151, 170-173; merger /…ģň§/+/√įň§/‚Üí/√įň§/ p.170). ŠļďńĀ ĺ ‚Üí [√įň§] (p.46:  ēa√įň§m 'bone', wa√įň§iňźfa 'job', ma√įň§alla). Vowels: short /i e a u o/, long /iňź eňź aňź oňź uňź/, [uňź] preserved, diphthongs aj/aw preserved (p.51); imńĀla aňź‚Üíeňź (weňźńßid, ńßeňźdiőł) / aňź‚Üíoňź ( Éoňźh, ńßayoňźh). m-article ~ l-article variation (Table 2.13, p.76). Explicitly treats RijńĀl Alma Ņ (Asiri 2009) as a separate neighbouring TihńĀmat- ŅAsńęr lect (p.30), and TihńĀmi tribal lects (TihńĀmi  ŅAsńęri, TihńĀmi QaŠł•tńĀni, TihńĀmi Ň†ahrńĀni) as 'different but mutually intelligible' (p.33).

sources.author: Watson, J.C.E. & Al-Azraqi, M.
sources.title: Lateral fricatives and lateral emphatics in southern Saudi Arabia and Mehri
sources.publisher: Proceedings of the Seminar for Arabian Studies 41, 425-432. Archaeopress, Oxford
sources.notes: Areal context for the lateral ŠłćńĀd/ŠļďńĀ ĺ across the  ŅAsńęr and TihńĀmah. Used here as the comparative anchor that distinguishes TihńĀmi QaŠł•tńĀni (ŠłćńĀd […ģň§]~[√įň§] merging; ŠļďńĀ ĺ [√įň§]) from RijńĀl Alma Ņ (ŠłćńĀd […ģň§] vs ŠļďńĀ ĺ VOICELESS lateral […¨ň§], kept distinct).

sources.author: Al-Azraqi, M.
sources.title: The ancient ŠłĆńĀd in southwest Saudi Arabia
sources.notes: Documents the survival of the lateral(ized) ŠłćńĀd across the  ŅAsńęr and TihńĀmah, corroborating the conservative […ģň§] reflex of ŠłćńĀd modelled here.

location.notes: TihńĀmat QaŠł•ŠĻ≠ńĀn,  ŅAsńęr Province, southwestern Saudi Arabia (the TihńĀmah escarpment/coastal-plain zone of the QaŠł•ŠĻ≠ńĀn tribe; approximate).

stress.notes: Quantity-sensitive Arabic stress, inherited from the peninsular parent; see ar-SA-x-najd for the cascade and input contract. Prosodic clitics as for the peninsular class.
